title: The type signature `{ → }` represents Precept's governing contract as a typed function. The curly braces are the definition scope — the boundaries within which the contract operates. The arrow inside is the governed transformation: every valid state change in Precept is a structurally typed transition. The braces are dim (the scope is structural, not flashy); the arrow is bright (the governing operator is what the definition certifies).
conceptualBridge: The `{ → }` type signature means Precept's definition is the typed scope of all valid transitions because curly braces mark a governed code scope and the arrow inside is the function that only runs when the type system approves.
decision: Kept at threshold (4.0): the type-signature aesthetic is new and code-specific; weak structural soundness score (3.5) is the main risk — the braces don't project the governed authority the way a filled solid or geometric form does.
